text: Sprint interval training (3-8 maximal sprints, 3 times per week for 4 weeks) improves 5-km run performance by 4.5% in untrained young men
Sprint interval training improves absolute maximum oxygen uptake by 4.9% over 4 weeks in untrained individuals
Sprint interval training requires substantially less training time and volume than traditional endurance training while still producing significant cardiorespiratory fitness gains
A progressive sprint interval protocol starting at 3 sprints and building to 8 sprints per session over 12 sessions is effective for improving 5-km performance
Improvements in VO2max from sprint interval training are associated with concurrent improvements in 5-km time trial performance
